raw.objective: “…to increase knowledge of PHT disposition by developing a population pharmacokinetic model of PHT using sparse, routine drug monitoring data obtained during intravenous and enteral administration of PHT of neonates and infants with seizures”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, gestational age (GA), birth weight (BWT), postnatal age (PNA), SEX, Apgar score at 1 minute (APG1) and 5 minutes (APG5), albumin, creatinine Included: WT and PNA on CL, WT on V
raw.observations: The model uses linear elimination instead of michaelis menten elimination, which may result in underexposure or overexposure based on their predicted dosing regimen. An interoccasional variability in clearance was estimated, which may in part be the result of not using michaelis menten elimination. The mpact of interoccasional variability was almost as large as the actual interindividual variability, which could imply that at each occasion, an individual is different from the last occasion.
raw.dose: Steady-state oral and IV doses in patients
raw.structure: 1-compartment model with zero-order infusion, first-order absorption and first-order elimination
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.829 theta2=74.2 theta3=0.167 theta4=0.757 theta5=0.0692 omega1=0.439 omega2=0.358 omega3=0.259 sigma1=0.232

structural_model.description: 1-compartment model with zero-order infusion, first-order absorption and first-order elimination

tested: Apgar score at 1 minute (APG1) and 5 minutes (APG5)
covariates.raw: Tested: WT, gestational age (GA), birth weight (BWT), postnatal age (PNA), SEX, Apgar score at 1 minute (APG1) and 5 minutes (APG5), albumin, creatinine Included: WT and PNA on CL, WT on V

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
